summary: schedule diff covers pumps, lights, relays (per-BoW and backyard-level), and chlorinator config changes; previously only heaters were compared.
reason: Greptile P1 #3216324918. The original diffMspConfigs only compared heater counts + setpoints + enable-state. The command's Long help promises to 'catch silent edits made by service techs or by other app users', but pumps/lights/relays/chlorinator changes silently produced an empty changes:[] — a service tech reconfiguring a VSP's speed range, repurposing a relay, or swapping a salt cell is exactly the kind of edit users need flagged. Fix: new diffEquipmentSet generic walker (count, added/removed, renamed, type-changed, function-changed, speed-range-changed, v2-active-changed), pointer-aware diffChlorinator (presence + CellType + Name + Type), extracted diffHeaters that now also reports heater-added/removed, and backyard-level relays diffed via cfg.Relays.
validated_outcome: TestDiffMspConfigs locks 10 scenarios: no-op, heater setpoint change, pump speed range change, pump renamed, pump removed, light V2 toggled, relay function repurposed, chlorinator cell-type swapped, chlorinator removed, backyard-level relay renamed.
